properties.remark: THIRD OF METAL ROOF TORN OFF NEAR LINCOLN AND OTTOWA
STALLION RNWY - WHITE SNDS MSSLE RNGE
UNAMED ARROYO FEEDING INTO THE SAN FRANCISCO RIVER BECAME FLOODED NEAR THE LUNA POST OFFICE. PROPERTY DAMAGE IN A NEARBY YARD ALSO REPORTED.
FLOODING ON A RANCH ALONG U.S. 70 AT MILE MARKER 268.
TORNADO VIEWED AT 520 PM MDT. LASTED LESS THAN A MINUTE.
*** 1 FATAL *** BARN ROOF COLLAPSED ONTO ELDERLY MAN

THERE WAS AN INITIAL PUSH OF FOUR FEET OF WATER AND DEBRIS IN PERALTA CANYON NEAR FOREST SERVICE ROAD 266 AROUND 2 PM MDT. BY 3 PM MDT... THE FLOW WAS TWO TO TWO AND A HALF FEET DEEP AND 15 TO 20 FEET WIDE.
FLOODING ON COUNTY ROAD 266 IN PERALTA CANYON. WATER AND DEBRIS COVERING THE ROAD.
SIGNIFICANT RUNOFF OF UNKNOWN DEPTH TRANSPORTED SIGNIFICANT ASH AND DEBRIS INTO LAKE MALOYA.
NORTH UNDERPASS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOOD. RATON CREEK SPILLING OVER.
WHITE SANDS MISSILE RANGE - STALLION RUNWAY.
FLOODING IN ALLISON CANYON AND OVER 3 FEET OF WATER IS FLOWING INTO THE RIVER RANCH RV PARK NEAR HWY 70
FLOODING AT HIGHLANDS AND JOE WELCH INTERSECTION. ESTIMATED 2 FEET DEEP.
FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS NOTED NEAR BUSINESSES ALONG HIGWAY 70.
FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS NOTED NEAR BUSINESSES ALONG HIGWAY 70.
FOREST SERVICE ROADS 120 B AND C FLOODED AND DAMAGED
EVACUATION OF RIVER RANCH RV PARK WITH WATER FLOWING FAST OUT OF GAVILAN CANYON AND RIO RUIDOSO OUT OF BANKS.
EVACUATION OF RIVER RANCH RV PARK WITH WATER FLOWING FAST OUT OF GAVILAN CANYON AND RIO RUIDOSO OUT OF BANKS.
WATER HALFWAY UP VEHICLES ALONG MALONEY AVENUE FROM FIRST TO SIXTH STREETS. SEVERAL INTERSECTIONS WERE CLOSED.
RATON CREEK APPROXIMATELY 30 FEET OUT OF IT BANKS ON THE NORTH SIDE OF TOWN. NO REPORTS OF SIGNIFICANT FLOOD DAMAGE AT THIS TIME.
STATE POLICE IN RATON REPORTED ROCK AND TREE LIMB DEBRIS ON INTERSTATE 25 FROM MILE MARKERS 457 TO 459. DOT WAS DISPATCHED TO CLEAN UP INCIDENT.

SANTA FE POLICE REPORTED HOME EVACUATIONS AROUND 1000 PM NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF VALMORA ROAD AND SERAFINA STREET. WATER WAS REPORTED TO BE TWO FEET DEEP AND 40 FEET WIDE. IN ADDITION...VEHICLES ARE STALLED IN TWO FEET OF WATER AT RODEO ROAD AND OLD PECOS TRAIL.
HIGH WATER FLOWS OUT OF CANYONS AND ARROYOS ON THE EAST AND SOUTH SIDE OF THE WHITE FIRE BURN SCAR.
PORTIONS OF THE RIO OSO TRAILER PARK UNDERWATER.
HIGHWAY 501 TEMPORARILY CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
TWO MEN WORKING IN SANTA CLARA CANYON WERE RESCUED BY HELICOPTER DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
HIGHWAY 30 TEMPORARILY CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
FLASH FLOODING NOTED ALONG FRIJOLES CREEK. TWO BARRIER WALLS AROUND THE BANDELIER NATIONAL MONUMENT VISITORS CENTER WERE OVERTOPPED. SEPTIC SYSTEM LIFT STATIN WAS INUNDATED.
STREET AND HOME FLOODING REPORTED AT VARIOUS LOCATIONS WITHIN SANTA FE.
ESTIMATES 10FT OF WATER AT LEAST A FOOTBALL FIELD WIDE.

THIRTY TO FIFTY PERCENT OF DIXON APPLE FARM WAS DESTROYED BY SUDDEN RUNOFF. TWO VEHICLES...A SEMI TRAILER...AND VAST AMOUNTS OF DEBRIS WERE SWEPT AWAY INTO COCHITI CANYON FROM LAS CONCHAS BURN SCAR FLOODING.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOUSE...NUMEROUS FENCES BLOW DOWN
MUD AND ROCKSLIDE ACROSS ROADWAY AT NM4 AND NM502
